title: Regarding the Agreement on Mutual Recognition with the Republic of Korea
bodyHtml:
As known, the "Regulation on the Mutual Recognition of Authorized Economic Operator Programs of the Republic of Turkey Ministry of Commerce and the Korea Customs Service of the Republic of Korea" was signed on June 9, 2014.

For our companies holding the Authorized Economic Operator (AEO) status to benefit from the facilities under the Mutual Recognition Agreement at the Korea Customs Service, the General Directorate of Customs has published a letter outlining the necessary steps:

  1. Informing Korean importers with whom our companies trade that they have the Authorized Economic Operator Status in Turkey,
  2. An application by Korean importers  to the Korea Customs Service for the issuance of a new Overseas Business Partner Code (OBPC) when presenting the information of certified exporter companies,
  3. If there is an OBPC issued on behalf of our companies but not associated with an AEO code, Korean importers must apply to the Korea Customs Service to update the missing AEO code.

After these arrangements, Korean importers can use the OBPC properly associated with the AEO code for import declarations.

In this context, our companies with the AEO status must inform Korean importers with whom they trade about their Authorized Economic Operator Status. Korean importers, when indicating the updated OBPC associated with the AEO code in their import declarations to the Korea Customs Service, enable our country's authorized economic operator companies to benefit from the facilities provided under this regulation.

Please find the attached letter regarding this matter.

http://portal.ugm.com.tr/shared/dosyaal.ashx?arsivid=475f6546-a697-44f1-9f33-5dfdce18def2&refno=REF2024_0197.rar
